mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate increases discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive or flat when UNRATE crosses 4.5 percent in >60 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >50 percent of cases during mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, leading to negative forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods consistently coincide with positive 20-day returns in >55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that severely compresses equity multiples, driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive returns in >60 percent of triggered instances, invalidating the stagflation compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, producing positive short-term equity returns as markets price in improved economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth consistently precedes negative 5-day returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals economic weakening, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing away from equities and negative quarterly returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by > 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: Payroll declines of this magnitude are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55 percent of cases, negating the rebalancing effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside stable or falling policy rates indicates real rate compression, which fuels equity valuation expansion and positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate combination yields negative returns in >65 percent of observations, breaking the real rate compression link.
